deep_snippets.text: ## Chapter 12 Mendel's Experiments and Heredity 12.1 Mendel’s Experiments and the Laws of Probability 12.2 Characteristics and Traits 12.3 Laws of Inheritance Genetics is the study of heredity. Johann Gregor Mendel set the framework for genetics long before chromosomes or genes had been identified, at a time when meiosis was not well understood. Mendel selected a simple biological system and conducted methodical, quantitative analyses using large sample sizes. Because of Mendel’s work, the fundamental principles of heredity were revealed. We now know that genes, carried on chromosomes, are the basic functional units of heredity with the capability to be replicated, expressed, or mutated. Today, the postulates put forth by Mendel form the basis of classical, or Mendelian, genetics. Not all traits are transmitted from parents to offspring according to Mendelian genetics, but Mendel’s experiments serve as an excellent starting point for thinking about inheritance. 12.1 Mendel’s Experiments and the Laws of Probability

12 • Mendel's Experiments and Heredity Access for free at openstax.org > **[Figure 259, Page 342]** A large illustration or diagram (469×700px) related to: FIGURE, generation, plants, true, breeding, dominant. This image appears on page 342 of the textbook. ![Figure 259 - A large illustration or diagram (469×700px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0259_bfd40639.png) developed a way to determine whether an organism that expressed a dominant trait was a heterozygote or a homozygote. Called the test cross, this technique is still used by plant and animal breeders. In a test cross, the dominant-expressing organism is crossed with an organism that is homozygous recessive for the same characteristic. If the dominant-expressing organism is a homozygote, then all F1 offspring will be heterozygotes expressing the dominant trait (Figure 12.5). Alternatively, if the dominant expressing organism is a heterozygote, the F1 offspring will exhibit a 1:1 ratio of heterozygotes and recessive homozygotes (Figure 12.5). The test cross further validates Mendel’s postulate that pairs of unit factors segregate equally.

> **[Figure 1125, Page 1411]** A figure or diagram (469×310px) related to: FIGURE, three, spined, stickleback, evolved, saltwater. This image appears on page 1411 of the textbook. ![Figure 1125 - A figure or diagram (469×310px) related to: FIGURE, three, s](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_1125_57bbb5c5.jpg)

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 分离定律

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物
- **学段**: 高中
- **年级**: 高中11年级
- **章节**: 遗传的基本规律
- **知识点**: 分离定律
- **课标版本**: 普通高中课程标准（2017年版2020年修订）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> （生物 11年级）分离定律的内容要求。学生应理解分离定律的基本概念，掌握基本方法，能运用所学知识解决简单的实际问题。

### 1.2 学业要求
> 学生能准确描述分离定律的相关概念，能进行基本的运算和推理，能解决与分离定律相关的实际问题，形成初步的生物思维和素养。

### 1.3 教学提示
> 教学时应注重从学生熟悉的生活情境入手，引导学生通过具体操作、观察、归纳等方式理解分离定律的概念。注重知识的发生发展过程，帮助学生建立知识之间的联系。

---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解分离定律的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解分离定律的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法
- 学生能够运用生物学学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验生物学探究的乐趣，树立学好生物学的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 分离定律的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构

- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
分离定律是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“概述DNA分子上的遗传信息通过RNA指导蛋白质的合成”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「分离定律」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“概述DNA分子上的遗传信息通过RNA指导蛋白质的合成”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: ## Chapter 12 Mendel's Experiments and Heredity 12.1 Mendel’s Experiments and the Laws of Probability 12.2 Characteristics and Traits 12.3 Laws of Inheritance Genetics is the study of heredity. Johann Gregor Mendel set the framework for genetics long before chromosomes or genes had been identified, at a time when meiosis was not well understood. Mendel selected a simple biological system and conducted methodical, quantitative analyses using large sample sizes. Because of Mendel’s work, the fundamental principles of heredity were revealed. We now know that genes, carried on chromosomes, are the basic functional units of heredity with the capability to be replicated, expressed, or mutated. Today, the postulates put forth by Mendel form the basis of classical, or Mendelian, genetics. Not all traits are transmitted from parents to offspring according to Mendelian genetics, but Mendel’s experiments serve as an excellent starting point for thinking about inheritance. 12.1 Mendel’s Experiments and the Laws of Probability

This image appears on page 342 of the textbook. ![Figure 259 - A large illustration or diagram (469×700px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0259_bfd40639.png) developed a way to determine whether an organism that expressed a dominant trait was a heterozygote or a homozygote. Called the test cross, this technique is still used by plant and animal breeders. In a test cross, the dominant-expressing organism is crossed with an organism that is homozygous recessive for the same characteristic. If the dominant-expressing organism is a homozygote, then all F1 offspring will be heterozygotes expressing the dominant trait (Figure 12.5). Alternatively, if the dominant expressing organism is a heterozygote, the F1 offspring will exhibit a 1:1 ratio of heterozygotes and recessive homozygotes (Figure 12.5). The test cross further validates Mendel’s postulate that pairs of unit factors segregate equally.

exercises.stem: 依据课标「分离定律」：概述DNA分子上的遗传信息通过RNA指导蛋白质的合成 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「分离定律」相关的生命现象。
